allies.rationale_text: Signaling helps distinguish context-matched proof from mere prestige transfer by asking what adoption evidence actually communicates underlying quality.
Authority-bias amplifies social-proof contagion when endorsement from high-status members spreads faster than direct evaluation.
allies.source_quote: opinions from high-status members are especially contagious

allies.rationale_text: Incentives clarify why copied behavior can drift from truth when actors are rewarded for prestige, affiliation, or conformity rather than causal accuracy.
allies.source_quote: context, incentives, or constraints do not match the current decision

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical-thinking resists social-proof pressure by asking for evidence quality and disconfirming analysis instead of external affirmation.
antagonists.source_quote: Social Proof encourages accepting an idea based on external affirmation, which runs counter to the objectives of Critical Thinking.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking conflicts with social proof by stripping away inherited group assumptions instead of accepting collective behavior as a starting point.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Social-proof conflicts with critical-thinking when external affirmation is allowed to replace direct evidence review and disconfirming analysis.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Social Proof encourages accepting an idea based on external affirmation, which runs counter to the objectives of Critical Thinking.

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays conservative around prestige contagion, authority amplification, evidence substitution, and the explicit source conflict with critical-thinking.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader conformity or norm-spread neighborhoods outside the current curated vocabulary.
open_questions: The source also supports cargo-cult pressure against causal analysis, but the kept set favors clearer current-vocabulary ids.
